sourceRef: tip:How to name files so your audit self thanks you

slides.headline: Master File Naming for Audits
slides.body: Learn how to name files to pass audits with flying colors.

slides.headline: Use the Year
slides.body: Always include the current year in file names to avoid confusion.
bullets: Ensure the year is always first

slides.headline: Add a Description
slides.body: Include a brief description to identify the file's content.

slides.headline: Include a Unique Identifier
slides.body: Add a unique identifier to track individual documents.

slides.headline: Follow the Rules
slides.body: Ensure your naming convention aligns with VA audit guidelines.
Apply these tips to your files now and pass your next audit with ease! Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Master file naming to pass audits with ease. Use the year, add a description, include a unique identifier. Follow 22-1999 and align with VA audit guidelines. Example: 20230101-VA-Enrollment-001. Apply these tips and pass your next audit with ease.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is the last one, which is more of a call to action rather than a clear takeaway. A stronger takeaway could be included to make it more impactful.
